title: malus sylvestris var. domestica
intro: Malus sylvestris var. domestica, also known as the wild apple, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Europe and Asia, known for its edible fruit and medicinal properties. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, particularly for its astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which are said to help soothe digestive issues and reduce swelling. The health benefits of Malus sylvestris var. domestica include its ability to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties. The plant's medicinal properties are commonly prepared in herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to promote overall well-being and alleviate various health issues.
